research_question: What short-form wellness video structures make quick home routines feel immediately doable?

analysis_method.frames_sampled: duration-aware frame sample, first 30 seconds emphasized

observations.opening_hook: The creator names a common time constraint before demonstrating anything.
observations.first_frame_pattern: Creator is already in the environment where the problem happens.
observations.visual_structure: Direct-to-camera hook, quick proof of constraint, three demonstrated moves, visible relaxed finish.
observations.spoken_or_text_framing: Language frames the routine as relief instead of discipline.
replicable_moves: Start in the real-life context instead of a studio setup.
Make the constraint visible with a clock, laptop, or object.
Show the emotional result in the same frame family as the opening.
avoid_notes: Do not copy the exact routine or caption language.
Avoid intensity claims that clash with Luna's no-shame positioning.

cross_video_patterns: The strongest quick-routine videos make the constraint visible before explaining the movement.
A same-location payoff helps viewers feel the transformation without needing platform-specific edits.
creator_fit_findings: Luna Fit should borrow the real-life constraint structure, not the specific routine.
Soft apartment visuals and direct-to-camera reassurance fit the observed high-clarity pattern.
